title: Playbook - Defender For Endpoint Device Inventory And Onboarding Counts
applies_to: Retrieve Defender for Endpoint machine inventory for automation or reporting
Count onboarded devices for security reports and dashboards
prerequisites: Defender for Endpoint app registration and token flow working
Machine.Read or Machine.Read.All-equivalent permission grant verified for the chosen auth mode
Filtering criteria such as onboarding status, device group, risk, or last seen date
required_permissions: Delegated flows additionally require a user role with at least view access and device group visibility
request_sequence: Acquire a token for the Defender for Endpoint API audience expected by the target API family.
Apply OData filters such as onboardingStatus, healthStatus, riskScore, or lastSeen where useful.
Follow pagination or iterate with $top and $skip as documented.
Compute onboarded-device counts from the filtered machine set or persist detailed machine rows for downstream reporting.
Fetch per-machine enrichment only when needed to avoid overfetching.

confirmed_facts.fact: Device inventory is exposed at GET /api/machines.
The machines API supports OData operators including $filter, $top, and $skip.
The machines API supports filtering on fields including computerDnsName, id, version, deviceValue, machineTags, onboardingStatus, lastSeen, and exposureLevel.
The machines API returns up to 10,000 devices by default with a maximum page size of 10,000.
Delegated access also depends on Defender role permissions and device group visibility, not only token scopes.

implementation_notes: For onboarded-device counts, filter server-side on onboardingStatus whenever the reporting question is specifically about onboarded devices.
Persist machine ID as the durable join key for later joins to alerts, vulnerabilities, recommendations, or device-group score data.
Use server-side filters first because machine inventories can become large.
For monthly reporting, store extracted snapshots or period-end counts; the machines API is a live inventory surface and not a built-in historical trend endpoint.
common_failures: 403 from missing Defender role visibility or permission grant.
Incomplete inventories when the client only reads the first page.
Confusing current live inventory with a historical month-end population count.
